column1: # mapping_set_title: CIS Controls v8.1 to NIST CSF 2.0 Schema Mappings

# mapping_set_description: Schema-level mappings between the CIS Critical Security Controls v8.1 LinkML schema and the NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 LinkML schema. Covers class, slot, and enum-value correspondences.

column10: Both are tree-root document containers wrapping a versioned cybersecurity framework publication
CIS Controls (18 items) are analogous in granularity to CSF Categories (second-level groupings within a Function); both group related specific actions
CIS Safeguards are specific measurable defensive actions, directly analogous to CSF Subcategories (specific cybersecurity outcomes)
The SecurityFunction enum enumerates exactly the six NIST CSF 2.0 Functions (GV, ID, PR, DE, RS, RC); enum as a whole corresponds to the CSFFunction class
CIS SecurityFunction.Govern is the CIS Controls representation of NIST CSF 2.0 Function GV (GOVERN), added in CSF 2.0
CIS SecurityFunction.Identify maps directly to NIST CSF 2.0 Function ID (IDENTIFY)
CIS SecurityFunction.Protect maps directly to NIST CSF 2.0 Function PR (PROTECT)
CIS SecurityFunction.Detect maps directly to NIST CSF 2.0 Function DE (DETECT)
CIS SecurityFunction.Respond maps directly to NIST CSF 2.0 Function RS (RESPOND)
CIS SecurityFunction.Recover maps directly to NIST CSF 2.0 Function RC (RECOVER)
Both are identifier slots serving as the primary key for catalog elements
Both are human-readable title slots mapped to dct:title
Both are version identifier slots for the document
Both carry free-text narrative content; CSF uses prose within structured CSFPart, CIS uses description as a flat string
CIS overview (control-level narrative) corresponds to the prose field of a CSFPart with name=overview
Both are inlined list slots holding child security elements (Safeguards vs CSFSubcategories) within a parent grouping
Both are the top-level collection slots in the root document — CISControlsDocument.controls (list of CISControl) mirrors CSFCatalogBody.groups (list of CSFFunction at one level up)
CIS IG tiers have no direct CSF equivalent; the closest analog is CSFProperty metadata on elements (props), but the semantics differ significantly
